mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate held near zero for an extended period compresses the equity discount rate and increases the relative attractiveness of stocks versus cash and short-duration bonds, producing a persistent upward drift in S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon regardless of short-term news flow.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 60-day forward SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or SPX exhibits negative average 60-day returns during periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Once the unemployment rate stops rising for two consecutive monthly readings after a prolonged uptrend, it signals labor market stabilization ahead of a broader recovery, prompting equity markets to price in improving corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE fails to make a new local high for 2 consecutive months after a prior 6-month uptrend
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 10.0 percent as of 2009-11, near cycle peak
regime_hint: labor market turning point after recession peak unemployment
failure_condition: In more than 40 percent of historical instances where UNRATE plateaus after a prolonged uptrend, 20-day forward SPX returns are negative, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month acceleration in industrial production and stabilization of nonfarm payroll declines jointly confirm that the real economy is exiting recession, a combined signal stronger than either series alone, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following month as investors reprice growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 99.44 rising from crisis trough, PAYEMS at 130996 thousand still declining but decelerating as of late 2009
failure_condition: Joint INDPRO acceleration and PAYEMS deceleration in decline fails to produce positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of historical occurrences, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over either series alone (partial Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts rebounding off a multi-decade low following a severe recession signal that a key demand-side cyclical sector is bottoming, which historically precedes a sustained equity market rally as construction, durable goods, and consumer spending chains reactivate over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 529 thousand units as of 2009-10, near historic lows
regime_hint: post-recession housing sector bottom and recovery
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns fail to be significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) following HOUST readings within 10 percent of trailing 24-month lows, across at least two historical recession recoveries.

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI inflation remains subdued (below 2 percent year-over-year) while the federal funds rate sits at the zero lower bound, the market anticipates a prolonged reflationary policy stance without near-term tightening risk, supporting risk asset valuations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year change < 2 percent and FEDFUNDS < 0.25 percent
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 216.385 with subdued inflation and FEDFUNDS at 0.12 percent as of late 2009
failure_condition: During periods meeting the low-CPI, near-zero-FEDFUNDS trigger, 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 45 percent of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The month-over-month rate of change in nonfarm payrolls, even while still negative, exhibits momentum that is priced incrementally by equity markets over short horizons; a decelerating pace of job losses produces small but consistent positive S&P 500 returns over the following week as it reduces tail-risk pricing of a deeper downturn.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130996 thousand as of 2009-11, monthly declines decelerating from 2009 peak losses
failure_condition: The correlation between the month-over-month change in PAYEMS deceleration and 5-day forward SPX returns is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts jointly indicate that both the consumer and residential investment channels of aggregate demand are recovering simultaneously, a stronger and more durable growth signal than either series alone, leading to sustained positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 352073 million dollars recovering post-crisis, HOUST at 529 thousand units near cycle lows as of late 2009
failure_condition: Joint positive month-over-month RSAFS and HOUST growth fails to produce 60-day forward SPX returns exceeding the unconditional historical average in more than 50 percent of occurrences, or interaction Rank IC < 0.02 versus single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: During an early-cycle industrial recovery regime, an acceleration in industrial production growth above a 3 percent annualized 3-month rate signals a durable supply-side expansion that translates into rising corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following month; the effect is expected to weaken or reverse in late-cycle or contractionary regimes where rapid IP growth instead signals overheating risk.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth rate exceeds 3 percent
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 99.4398 as of 2009-11, rebounding from 2009 trough
regime_hint: early-cycle industrial recovery following recession trough
failure_condition: In late-cycle or high-inflation regimes, INDPRO acceleration above the 3 percent threshold is associated with flat or negative 20-day forward SPX returns in over 50 percent of instances, invalidating the regime-conditional direction; or Rank IC < 0.03 in the early-cycle regime specifically over any 2-year window.
